notes: Two turns separated by a `/model set gemini-2.5-flash` slash. Turn 1 runs under model A (the account's OAuth boot model — the gemini driver has no --model pin, so A is environment-dependent); the mid-session switch uses gemini-cli's INLINE-ARGUMENT form `/model set <name>` (NOT the arrow-key picker — recordable via a plain `slash` step), so turn 2 runs under model B (gemini-2.5-flash). The transcript carries two distinct message.model values, one per turn. MergeMetrics' 'latest non-empty/non-unknown wins' rule (session.go L627-628) promotes B after turn 2: turn 1 attributes to A (PerTurnContribution.Model=A), turn 2 to B (Model=B), and SessionMetrics.ModelName flips to B by t2_end. TotalTokens accumulates across both models (T2 > T1). The `/model set` slash does no LLM round-trip and writes no turn message, so no state machinery fires in the switch window — model_switch_window asserts that gap with duration_at_least_ms and 'no working transition' invariants. The concrete boot model A is account/rollout-dependent; the durable assertion is that the model CHANGES between turns and a non-empty model B is latest at session end (issue #440), not a hardcoded pair. The switch is session-only (no --persist), so the account default is untouched.

text: Turn 1 user prompt -> working under model A (the OAuth boot model)
Turn 1 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model A
/model set slash runs locally (no LLM round-trip, no turn message); state stays ready, no working flicker
Turn 2 user prompt -> working under model B (gemini-2.5-flash)
Turn 2 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model B (latest wins); TotalTokens after T2 > TotalTokens after T1
